| Attachment No. 1

2 1. Cross-Complainant University of San Francisco (“the University” or “Cross-

3 || Complainant”) alleges as follows:

4 2. University of San Francisco is, and at all times herein relevant was, a not-for-

5 ||profit, public benefit corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of

6 || Califomia, with its principal place of business in the City and County of San Francisco,

7 || California.

8 3. During the period June 1, 1999 to May 31, 2002, Cross-Defendant Patricia

9 || Westerfield (“Cross-Defendant”) was an employee of the University, with all duties and
10 |} obligations to her employer, the University, as provided by law and the contractual terms and
11 |} conditions of her employment. Cross-Defendant was an employee employed by the University
12 |jin the department known as the University’s Disability Related Services Office (DRS). DRS
13 || supports the University’s efforts to provide services to its undergraduate and graduate students
14 || who have physical, emotional, psychiatric and other mixed-medical disabilities, as defined by
15 ||law. The University has an extensive program to assess student requests for reasonable
16 || academic and other accommodations for such disabilities.
17 4. DRS handles highly confidential medical information about University students,
18 |} including psychiatric information about students, information about physical diseases and other
19 ||private medical facts. DRS assures students that their medical information will remain
20 || confidential and will not leave the University. Without that assurance, students with disabilities
21 || would feel reluctant to come to the DRS and the University could not carry out its duty to assist
22 || them.
23 5. DRS and the University have strict policies against breach by University staff
24 ||members (such as Cross-Defendant) of the confidentiality of student medical information.
25 || Extensive psychiatric, physical disease, emotional and other private and personal health and
26 || other information stored in DRS solely is to be used for providing services to those University
27 || students. Cross-Defendant was fully aware of these policies and rules and, agreed to orally and
28 || in writing abide by them, but she breached that agreement.

‘UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO v. WESTERFIELD [SESC #CGC 03-417577] ATTACHMENT NO.

 
1 6. In or about March 2002, April 2002, and/or May 2002, Cross-Defendant secretly,
2 ||surreptiously, and without authorization from the University, recorded private medical
3 [information of dozens of University students, copied their confidential medical documents,
4 || manufactured documents containing the names, social security numbers and medical conditions
5 || (psychiatric, physical, other) of such students, and then, in an effort to prevent the University
6 || and/or the students from discovering her actions, deleted from University computers, electronic
7 |{ documents containing evidence of these acts, and then permanently removed to her possession,
8 ||at her home and other locations to be discovered, such records, documents and information
9 || (herein “materials I”); and has thereafter at all times, failed and refused to return such materials
10 |[to the University and to provide an accounting to the University of all locations, disclosures and
11 || copies of such materials.
12 7. In or about March 2002, April 2002, and/or May 2002, Cross-Defendant secretly,
13 || surreptiously, and without authorization from the University, accessed records, business
14 |}documents and personnel information of the University, and then removed to her possession, at
15 || her home and other locations to be discovered, such information, and has thereafter at all times,
16 |} failed and refused to return such information to the University and to provide an accounting to
17 || the University of all locations, disclosures, originals and copies of such information (hereafter
18 {| called “materials I”) that have been in the possession of Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents
19 || and representatives.
20 8. Cross-Complainant will seek leave to amend as Cross-Defendants those other
21 ||individuals, attorneys, agents and representatives of Cross-Defendant, who are in receipt,
22 || possession or control of the University materials I and II, to be discovered.
23
ry

26 || through 8, above.
27 10. University of San Francisco seeks a declaration: (a) of its rights to sole
28 || possession of the materials I and II; (b) that Cross-Defendant violated her obligations to the

7 For Prohibitory and Mandatory Injunctive Relief

9 || through 8, above.
10 12. University of San Francisco seeks an order of the Court: (a) requiring Cross-
11 || Defendant, her attorneys, agents and representatives, immediately to provide a written
12 || accounting, under oath, of all of the current and past locations of materials I and II, and as to
13 || which Cross-Defendant has permitted other individuals to access and/or copy; (b) requiring
14 ||Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents and representatives, immediately to return to the
15 || University, accompanied by an inventory thereof executed under oath, the originals and all
16 ||copies of materials I and II; and enjoining Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents and
17 || representatives, from further disclosing any and all materials I and II.

c. Defendant concealed or suppressed these facts with the intent to defraud and induce plaintiff to act
as described in item FR-5. At the time plaintiff acted, plaintiff was unaware of the concealed or suppressed
facts and would not have taken the action if plaintiff had known the facts.

FR-4. (_] Promise Without Intent to Perform

a. Defendant made a promise about a material matter without any intention of performing it (-) as stated
in Attachment FR-4.a [Jas follows:

b. Defendant's promise without any intention of performance was made with the intent to defraud and induce
plaintiff to rely upon it and to act as described in item FR-5. At the time plaintiff acted, plaintiff was
unaware of defendant's intention not to perform the promise. Plaintiff acted in justifiable reliance upon
the promise.

FR-5. In justifiable reliance upon defendant's conduct, plaintiff was Induced to act [[] as stated in Attachment FR-5
[x1 as follows:
University of San Francisco was induced to refrain from discharging Plaintiff for misconduct.
University of San Francisco was induced, by ignorance of the concealed facts, to not take steps to
recover materials I and/or II.
FR-6. Because of plaintiffs reliance upon defendant's conduct, plaintiff has been damaged CC] as stated in
AttachmentFR-6 [X] as follows:
(1) University of San Francisco would have not been required to defend Cross-Defendant's complaint
in this action and to expend sums for costs and attorneys fees in defense.
(2) University of San Francisco would have had information needed to defend cross-defendant's
complaint.
(3) University of San Francisco would have possession and use of its property, materials I and II.
